required_inputs.role: Respondent-item file for the four economic and vocational ANX items, validated against schema/wave_response.schema.json before any item distributions or psychometric models are estimated.
required_inputs.validation_rule: All rows must use wave_id anx_us_2026w05_economic, benchmark_version v0.5.0, item_version v0.1.0, event_id no_event, and exactly the four frozen economic_vocational_anxiety item IDs.

required_inputs.role: Respondent-level analytic file containing sample split, eligibility, exclusions, quotas, weights, demographics, employment variables, AI exposure variables, baseline anxiety, external-validity comparators, and paradata.
required_inputs.validation_rule: Each respondent must appear in exactly one non-overlapping sample_id, all eligible respondents must join to four administered item rows unless item missingness is explicitly coded, and all preregistered exclusion variables must be present before outcome inspection.

required_inputs.role: Frozen item wording, response anchors, scoring keys, construct metadata, exclusion metadata, and validation metadata for the administered economic and vocational item pool.
required_inputs.validation_rule: The analysis allowlist is exactly skill_obsolescence_software, wage_pressure_customer_support, retraining_pressure_accounting, and status_loss_creative_work, all with item_version v0.1.0 and construct_id economic_vocational_anxiety.

required_inputs.role: Frozen codebook, instrument, preregistration, and no-event registry used to audit variable derivations, item administration, event-study prohibitions, and public-data restrictions.
required_inputs.validation_rule: Analysis scripts must reject renamed or recoded codebook variables unless a dated preregistration addendum was filed before outcome inspection and preserves all pass-fail decisions.

packages.name: psych
packages.purpose: Polychoric correlations, ordinal EFA, corrected item-total correlations, omega summaries, and item descriptive diagnostics.

packages.name: GPArotation
packages.purpose: Oblique exploratory factor rotations for pilot factor-structure diagnostics.

packages.name: lavaan
packages.purpose: Ordered-categorical CFA and multi-group measurement-invariance models using WLSMV.

packages.name: semTools
packages.purpose: Ordinal reliability helpers, CFA fit summaries, and measurement-invariance comparison tables.

packages.name: mirt
packages.purpose: Graded-response IRT calibration, item information, threshold ordering checks, local dependence, and DIF sensitivity models.

packages.name: lordif
packages.purpose: Ordinal logistic DIF screening with effect-size diagnostics for demographic, employment, AI exposure, and mode subgroups.

packages.name: survey
packages.purpose: Post-stratified weighted descriptive distributions, weight diagnostics, and weighted sensitivity estimates.

packages.name: mice
packages.purpose: Covariate-only sensitivity checks for external-validity models when non-ANX covariates are missing.

software_environment.version_lock_rule: Analyses must archive sessionInfo output and package versions exactly; any substitution requires a preregistered addendum showing unchanged confirmatory pass-fail decisions.

randomization_and_seeds.seed_policy: Seeds are fixed before outcome inspection, set immediately before stochastic procedures, and written to the validation dossier with the exact R session lock.

sample_splits.overlap_rule: A respondent assigned to the development pilot is permanently ineligible for the independent confirmation sample and must not contribute to confirmation pass-fail decisions.
The confirmation sample must contain no respondent, panel identifier, household linkage, or protected linkage token appearing in the development pilot.

eligibility_rules.rule: Respondent is age 18 or older, resides in the United States, can complete the survey in English without assistance, and provided informed consent.
Confirmation respondents must not have participated in the development pilot or share protected linkage evidence with a pilot respondent.

exclusion_rules.rule: Exclude respondents who do not select Moderately anxious on the instructed-response attention check.
Exclude respondents who fail the scenario-comprehension check identifying the scenarios as hypothetical research materials.
Exclude respondents who fail the economic-vocational attribution check about rating anxiety toward economic or vocational changes.
Exclude respondents whose completion time is below one-third of the same-sample median after clear breakoffs are removed.
Exclude respondents who give the same substantive response to all four ANX items and also fail an attention or minimum-reading-time check.
Exclude respondents missing or giving non-substantive responses on more than 25 percent of administered ANX items.
Exclude confirmed duplicate respondents, non-consenting respondents, protocol-ineligible respondents, breakoffs without usable item data, vendor-confirmed fraud, and records unusable after audit.
Exclude respondents reporting that they understood only a few scenarios or did not understand the scenarios.

non_exclusion_flags.rule: A single item response time below one-third of the sample median item time is retained as a sensitivity flag and does not exclude by itself.
Unemployment, low income, career transition, high perceived occupational AI exposure, and high baseline general anxiety are retained for DIF or validity analyses and do not exclude by themselves.
A vendor low-confidence flag that is not confirmed as fraud remains a sensitivity flag and does not exclude by itself.

weighting_policy.primary_estimation: Primary EFA, CFA, omega, IRT, DIF, invariance, and external-validity pass-fail decisions are unweighted unless the estimator has a validated survey-weight implementation.
weighting_policy.descriptive_estimation: Weighted and unweighted item distributions, missingness rates, sample characteristics, and exclusion rates must both be reported for the pilot and confirmation sample.
weighting_policy.weight_construction: Construct post-stratification or raking weights separately by sample using age group, gender, race and ethnicity, education, Census region, employment status, and stable AI exposure or occupation margins when available.

trimming.exception_rule: Weights outside 0.30 to 3.00 must be trimmed unless a documented sensitivity analysis shows a wider range is necessary and leaves primary conclusions unchanged.

missing_data_handling.item_distributions: Report observed, skipped, breakoff, technical failure, and quality-control missingness separately for each item before respondent exclusions and after analytic exclusions.
missing_data_handling.factor_models: EFA and CFA use observed ordered responses from analytically eligible respondents; pairwise polychoric correlations are allowed for pilot EFA diagnostics, while confirmation CFA uses the estimator default for ordered categorical missingness without imputing ANX responses.
missing_data_handling.reliability: Ordinal omega and corrected item-total correlations are computed from eligible respondents with sufficient observed retained items; no ANX item response is imputed for pass-fail reliability gates.
missing_data_handling.irt: Graded-response IRT uses observed ordered item responses only; respondents with missing retained items contribute according to the package likelihood implementation and missingness must be reported.
missing_data_handling.external_validity: External-validity models use complete observed retained item summaries and prespecified covariates; missing non-ANX covariates may use explicit missing categories or covariate-only sensitivity imputation.
missing_data_handling.imputation_policy: Do not impute ANX item responses, item scores, construct summaries, exclusion flags, sample membership, or event fields; any covariate-only imputation is sensitivity evidence and cannot rescue a failed primary gate.

efa.estimator: Ordinal EFA on polychoric correlations with oblique rotation and parallel-analysis support.

pass_fail_thresholds.pass_action: Carry item forward to confirmation review.
pass_fail_thresholds.fail_action: Flag item for revision, exclusion, or item-level-only review.

pass_fail_thresholds.pass_action: No material secondary loading is detected.
pass_fail_thresholds.fail_action: Require written construct interpretation or block construct scoring.

pass_fail_thresholds.pass_action: Retain one-factor model or justify two-factor interpretation.
pass_fail_thresholds.fail_action: Block construct scoring because the four-item pool lacks coherent dimensionality.

cfa.estimator: Ordered-categorical CFA using WLSMV with the preregistered one-factor economic_vocational_anxiety model.

pass_fail_thresholds.pass_action: CFA CFI meets strong fit support.
pass_fail_thresholds.fail_action: If below 0.90 block construct scoring; if 0.90 to 0.949 require dossier caution.

pass_fail_thresholds.pass_action: CFA TLI meets strong fit support.
pass_fail_thresholds.fail_action: If below 0.90 block construct scoring; if 0.90 to 0.949 require dossier caution.

pass_fail_thresholds.pass_action: CFA RMSEA meets strong fit support.
pass_fail_thresholds.fail_action: If above 0.080 block construct scoring; if 0.061 to 0.080 require dossier caution.

pass_fail_thresholds.pass_action: CFA SRMR meets strong fit support.
pass_fail_thresholds.fail_action: Block or caution according to full fit pattern and residual diagnostics.

pass_fail_thresholds.pass_action: All retained items meet loading threshold.
pass_fail_thresholds.fail_action: Drop weak item or block construct scoring if fewer than three items remain.

omega.estimator: Ordinal omega from polychoric factor model with bootstrap confidence intervals where feasible.

pass_fail_thresholds.pass_action: Reliability is sufficient for early scored construct review.
pass_fail_thresholds.fail_action: Block construct scoring and require revision or additional items.

pass_fail_thresholds.pass_action: Reliability meets mature headline scoring review threshold.
pass_fail_thresholds.fail_action: Do not claim mature headline scoring readiness.

pass_fail_thresholds.pass_action: Corrected item-total correlations support retention.
pass_fail_thresholds.fail_action: Require item-level justification or exclusion.

irt.estimator: Unidimensional graded-response IRT model for ordered 1 to 5 item responses.

pass_fail_thresholds.pass_action: All retained items show minimally useful discrimination.
pass_fail_thresholds.fail_action: Flag weak item for revision, exclusion, or claim blocking.

pass_fail_thresholds.pass_action: Response categories function in ordered severity sequence.
pass_fail_thresholds.fail_action: Investigate response scale functioning and block scored approval if unresolved.

pass_fail_thresholds.pass_action: No severe residual local dependence is detected.
pass_fail_thresholds.fail_action: Block construct scoring or revise redundant items.

dif.estimator: Ordinal logistic DIF with Benjamini-Hochberg FDR control within the four-item family and IRT DIF sensitivity where subgroup sizes permit.

pass_fail_thresholds.pass_action: No statistically flagged DIF remains after family control.
pass_fail_thresholds.fail_action: Evaluate practical DIF before retention decision.

pass_fail_thresholds.pass_action: Practical DIF by pseudo R-squared is below threshold.
pass_fail_thresholds.fail_action: Block scored approval unless justified as immaterial and disclosed.

pass_fail_thresholds.pass_action: Expected score impact is below material threshold.
pass_fail_thresholds.fail_action: Block or limit claims for affected subgroup comparisons.

invariance.estimator: Multi-group ordered-categorical CFA for configural, metric, and scalar or threshold invariance, with preregistered IRT linking fallback if CFA is unstable.

pass_fail_thresholds.pass_action: Proceed to metric and scalar invariance comparisons.
pass_fail_thresholds.fail_action: Block subgroup comparability claims for affected grouping variable.

pass_fail_thresholds.pass_action: Metric invariance CFI threshold passes.
pass_fail_thresholds.fail_action: Flag metric non-invariance for the grouping variable.

pass_fail_thresholds.pass_action: Metric invariance RMSEA threshold passes.
pass_fail_thresholds.fail_action: Flag metric non-invariance for the grouping variable.

pass_fail_thresholds.pass_action: Scalar or threshold invariance CFI threshold passes.
pass_fail_thresholds.fail_action: Block mean-comparison claims for affected grouping variable.

pass_fail_thresholds.pass_action: Scalar or threshold invariance RMSEA threshold passes.
pass_fail_thresholds.fail_action: Block mean-comparison claims for affected grouping variable.

external_validity.estimator: Spearman correlations, ordinal regression, and adjusted incremental-validity models using retained economic_vocational_anxiety summaries only after dimensionality and reliability gates pass.

pass_fail_thresholds.pass_action: Convergent validity with perceived occupational AI exposure is in the preregistered range.
pass_fail_thresholds.fail_action: Require dossier explanation and block strong convergent-validity claims.

pass_fail_thresholds.pass_action: Retained score remains distinguishable from baseline general anxiety.
pass_fail_thresholds.fail_action: Block scored approval unless incremental AI-specific variance is demonstrated.

pass_fail_thresholds.pass_action: Labor-market worry association reaches practical threshold.
pass_fail_thresholds.fail_action: Block strong criterion-validity claim for labor-market worry.

pass_fail_thresholds.pass_action: Incremental validity reaches preregistered practical threshold.
pass_fail_thresholds.fail_action: Block incremental-validity claim or require coefficient-based sensitivity support.

validation_gates.pass_action: A construct-level future scoring review may continue.
validation_gates.fail_action: Block construct scoring and restrict any future use to item-level development reporting.

validation_gates.pass_action: Item missingness is acceptable for scored review.
validation_gates.fail_action: Flag item for implementation review or exclusion.
Block subgroup claims or require restricted-data review.

validation_gates.pass_action: No item is dominated by a single response category.
validation_gates.fail_action: Flag item for targeting or wording revision.

validation_gates.pass_action: No item is excessively concentrated in adjacent response categories.
validation_gates.fail_action: Require IRT information justification or block scored approval.

validation_gates.pass_action: Keep v0.5.0 non-scored because no observed validation dossier is approved at freeze.
validation_gates.fail_action: Do not enable scoring in v0.5.0; observed validation belongs in a later citable release.

sensitivity_analyses.description: Compare unweighted primary psychometric decisions with weighted descriptive and external-validity estimates using final trimmed post-stratification weights.
sensitivity_analyses.primary_comparison: Any conclusion that changes under weighted sensitivity must be flagged in the validation dossier and cannot be hidden behind the unweighted primary estimate.

sensitivity_analyses.description: Repeat item distributions, factor summaries, and reliability after excluding respondents with item-level low reading-time flags that were not primary exclusions.
sensitivity_analyses.primary_comparison: If retained item decisions change after low-reading-time sensitivity exclusions, the item is flagged for implementation review.

sensitivity_analyses.description: Repeat external-validity models using explicit missing categories and covariate-only imputation for non-ANX covariates.
sensitivity_analyses.primary_comparison: External-validity support must be stable in sign and practical interpretation across complete-case and covariate-missingness sensitivity specifications.

sensitivity_analyses.description: Repeat DIF and invariance screens after collapsing sparse public categories according to the codebook disclosure plan.
sensitivity_analyses.primary_comparison: A claim is subgroup-comparable only when sparse-cell handling does not introduce meaningful DIF or failed invariance.

dossier_output.write_policy: The dossier must be written only after fielding and must copy all preregistered statistics, thresholds, pass-fail labels, exclusions, weights, seeds, package versions, and reviewer decisions without post hoc renaming.

statistic_to_dossier_field.decision_use: Determines pilot item retention and whether weak items can enter confirmation review.
Determines whether one-factor confirmation evidence provides strong, cautionary, or blocking model fit support.
Determines whether the retained item set meets early and mature reliability review thresholds.
Determines whether retained items provide minimally useful graded-response discrimination.
Determines whether statistically detected subgroup DIF is practically material for retention or claim blocking.
Determines whether subgroup mean-comparison claims are permitted for each grouping variable.
Determines whether retained economic and vocational anxiety explains labor-market criteria beyond prespecified covariates.
Determines whether a later citable release can consider construct-level economic scoring.
Keeps v0.5.0 non-scored and requires any economic scoring to await observed validation in a later release.
